name: OTLP Span Subscriber
description: gRPC server on configured port (default 4317) receiving OTLP spans from Baton's adapter layer. Uses grpcio thread pool (AS002 — threading OK, no asyncio). On span receipt: enriches with arbiter.* attributes (C012 — arbiter.access.declared, arbiter.access.observed, arbiter.consistency, arbiter.trust.score, arbiter.trust.tier, arbiter.authority.domains, arbiter.taint.detected, arbiter.blast.tier, arbiter.conflict), then dispatches to consistency analyzer, access auditor, taint detector, and conflict resolver. Defines expected span schema contract with Baton (rabbit hole patch — validates span structure, logs warnings on unexpected format). Manages execution-ID correlation buffer for conflict detection windowing. Seven-layer design: (1) gRPC Transport with TraceServiceServicer, ThreadPoolExecutor, partial_success semantics, health checking; (2) Anti-Corruption/Schema Validation with BatonSpanContract Pydantic model, protobuf→domain conversion at handler boundary, custom TraceId/SpanId/ExecutionId types, structured warning logging, explicit default-value handling; (3) Enrichment Model with frozen ArbiterEnrichment composed into EnrichedSpan; (4) Protocol-based Dispatcher with SpanAnalyzer Protocol, 4 registered analyzers with failure isolation, result aggregation; (5) Correlation Buffer with bounded dict, threading.Lock, lazy+periodic eviction, metrics; (6) Configuration via OtlpSubscriberConfig Pydantic model from YAML; (7) Lifecycle with start/stop/is_ready and 3-second cold start target.

types.name: TraceId
types.description: 32-character lowercase hexadecimal string representing an OpenTelemetry trace ID. Must be exactly 32 hex chars and not all zeros.

types.name: SpanId
types.description: 16-character lowercase hexadecimal string representing an OpenTelemetry span ID. Must be exactly 16 hex chars and not all zeros.

types.name: ExecutionId
types.description: Non-empty string identifying a Baton execution context. Used as correlation key for conflict detection windowing. Typically a UUID or structured identifier from the adapter layer.

types.name: TrustTier
types.description: Trust tier enum. Used only for display/enrichment labeling — all policy calculations use raw trust score, never tiers (domain rule).

types.name: BlastTier
types.description: Blast radius classification tier for enrichment.

types.name: AnalyzerKind
types.description: Identifies which of the 4 registered downstream analyzers produced a result or error.

types.name: ServerStatus
types.description: gRPC server lifecycle status for health checking.

types.name: ValidationSeverity
types.description: Severity of span schema validation issues.

types.name: OtlpSubscriberConfig

fields.name: port
fields.description: gRPC listen port.

fields.name: max_workers
fields.description: ThreadPoolExecutor max_workers for gRPC server (AS002 — threading, no asyncio).

fields.name: buffer_max_size
fields.description: Maximum number of ExecutionId keys in the correlation buffer before eviction is forced.

fields.name: buffer_window_seconds
fields.description: Duration in seconds to retain spans in correlation buffer for conflict detection windowing.

validators.error_message: buffer_window_seconds must be between 1 and 300 seconds

fields.name: grace_shutdown_seconds
fields.description: Grace period in seconds for gRPC server shutdown. Allows in-flight RPCs to complete.

validators.error_message: grace_shutdown_seconds must be between 0 and 60 seconds

fields.name: eviction_sweep_interval_seconds
fields.description: Interval in seconds between periodic eviction sweeps of the correlation buffer by daemon thread.

validators.error_message: eviction_sweep_interval_seconds must be between 0.5 and 60 seconds

fields.name: max_batch_size
fields.description: Maximum number of spans accepted per ExportTraceServiceRequest. Batches exceeding this are partially accepted.

fields.name: bind_address
fields.description: Network address to bind the gRPC server to.

types.description: Pydantic model for OTLP subscriber configuration. Loaded from YAML via project config layer.

types.name: SpanAttribute

fields.name: key
fields.description: Attribute key.

fields.name: value
fields.description: Attribute value coerced to string representation. Protobuf default-value ambiguity handled: empty string means explicitly empty, None means attribute was absent.

fields.name: original_type
fields.description: Original protobuf AnyValue type name (string_value, int_value, bool_value, double_value, array_value, kvlist_value, bytes_value) for downstream type recovery.

types.description: A single key-value attribute from an OTLP span, converted from protobuf AnyValue to a Python-native representation.

types.name: SpanLink

fields.name: trace_id
fields.description: Trace ID of the linked span.

fields.name: span_id
fields.description: Span ID of the linked span.

fields.name: attributes
fields.description: Attributes on the link.

types.description: A link from one span to another, preserving trace/span context.

types.name: SpanEvent

fields.name: name
fields.description: Event name.

fields.name: timestamp_unix_nano
fields.description: Event timestamp in nanoseconds since Unix epoch (UTC).

fields.name: attributes
fields.description: Attributes on the event.

types.description: A timestamped event within a span.

types.name: SpanAttributeList
types.description: List of SpanAttribute.

types.name: SpanLinkList
types.description: List of SpanLink.

types.name: SpanEventList
types.description: List of SpanEvent.

types.name: ValidationIssue

fields.name: severity
fields.description: Whether this is a warning (span still processable) or error (span rejected).

fields.name: field
fields.description: Dot-path to the problematic field (e.g. 'attributes.baton.node_id').

fields.name: message
fields.description: Human-readable description of the issue, must include specific node/field/domain.

fields.name: span_id
fields.description: SpanId of the problematic span if available, empty string if not yet parsed.

fields.name: node_id
fields.description: Baton node ID if identifiable from span context, empty string otherwise.

types.description: A single schema validation issue found when converting a protobuf span to domain types. Includes specific node/field per project error message standards.

types.name: ValidationIssueList
types.description: List of ValidationIssue.

types.name: BatonSpanContract

fields.name: trace_id
fields.description: 32-char hex trace ID from OTLP span.

validators.error_message: trace_id must be exactly 32 lowercase hex characters
trace_id must not be all zeros

fields.name: span_id
fields.description: 16-char hex span ID from OTLP span.

validators.error_message: span_id must be exactly 16 lowercase hex characters
span_id must not be all zeros

fields.name: parent_span_id
fields.description: Parent span ID if this span has a parent. None for root spans.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Span operation name.

validators.error_message: Span name must be between 1 and 1024 characters

fields.name: start_time_unix_nano
fields.description: Span start time in nanoseconds since Unix epoch (UTC). Must be positive (not protobuf default 0).

validators.error_message: start_time_unix_nano must be positive, not protobuf default 0

fields.name: end_time_unix_nano
fields.description: Span end time in nanoseconds since Unix epoch (UTC). Must be >= start_time_unix_nano.

validators.error_message: end_time_unix_nano must be positive, not protobuf default 0

fields.name: attributes
fields.description: All span attributes converted from protobuf AnyValue to SpanAttribute.

fields.name: events
fields.description: Span events (timeline annotations).

fields.name: links
fields.description: Span links to other trace contexts.

fields.name: baton_node_id
fields.description: Baton adapter node identifier extracted from span attributes. Error if missing — adapter layer is ground truth.

validators.error_message: baton_node_id is required — adapter layer is ground truth for node identity

fields.name: baton_execution_id
fields.description: Execution context ID from Baton adapter for correlation buffer keying.

validators.error_message: baton_execution_id is required for conflict detection correlation

fields.name: baton_component_id
fields.description: Optional Pact component identifier from adapter attributes.

fields.name: baton_circuit_id
fields.description: Optional Pact circuit identifier from adapter attributes.

fields.name: resource_attributes
fields.description: Resource-level attributes from the OTLP ResourceSpans envelope.

types.description: Pydantic model defining the expected schema for Baton-originated spans at the anti-corruption boundary. Protobuf is converted to this model at the handler boundary; all downstream code uses domain types only. Handles protobuf default-value ambiguity: 0/empty != unset via Optional wrappers.

types.name: OptionalSpanId
types.description: Optional SpanId — None for root spans, present for child spans.

types.name: ParsedSpan

fields.name: trace_id
fields.description: Validated trace ID.

fields.name: span_id
fields.description: Validated span ID.

fields.name: parent_span_id
fields.description: Parent span ID or None for root.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Span operation name.

fields.name: start_time_unix_nano
fields.description: Span start timestamp in nanoseconds since Unix epoch (UTC).

fields.name: end_time_unix_nano
fields.description: Span end timestamp in nanoseconds since Unix epoch (UTC).

fields.name: duration_ns
fields.description: Computed span duration in nanoseconds (end - start). Always >= 0.

fields.name: attributes
fields.description: Span attributes in domain form.

fields.name: events
fields.description: Span events.

fields.name: links
fields.description: Span links.

fields.name: baton_node_id
fields.description: Baton adapter node ID — ground truth.

fields.name: baton_execution_id
fields.description: Execution context ID for correlation.

fields.name: baton_component_id
fields.description: Pact component ID if present.

fields.name: baton_circuit_id
fields.description: Pact circuit ID if present.

fields.name: resource_attributes
fields.description: Resource-level attributes.

fields.name: validation_issues
fields.description: Non-fatal validation issues (warnings) encountered during parsing. Empty if clean.

fields.name: received_at_unix_nano
fields.description: Timestamp when this span was received by the subscriber, in nanoseconds since Unix epoch (UTC). Set at handler boundary.

types.description: Domain model for a validated and parsed Baton span, produced from BatonSpanContract after schema validation passes. This is the type consumed by all downstream analyzers.

types.name: AnalysisResult

fields.name: analyzer_kind
fields.description: Which analyzer produced this result.

fields.name: success
fields.description: Whether the analyzer completed without internal error.

fields.name: error_message
fields.description: Error detail if success is false. Includes specific node/field per project standards.

fields.name: consistency_score
fields.description: Consistency score from consistency analyzer. Range [0.0, 1.0].

fields.name: access_declared
fields.description: Declared access pattern from access auditor.

fields.name: access_observed
fields.description: Observed access pattern from access auditor.

fields.name: authority_domains
fields.description: Authority domains from access auditor. Authority is declared, distinct from trust.

fields.name: trust_score
fields.description: Raw trust score. All policy calculations use raw score, never display tiers.

fields.name: trust_tier
fields.description: Trust tier for display/enrichment only — never used in policy calculations.

fields.name: taint_detected
fields.description: Whether taint was detected by taint detector.

fields.name: taint_details
fields.description: Detail of taint if detected.

fields.name: blast_tier
fields.description: Blast radius tier classification.

fields.name: conflict_description
fields.description: Conflict description from conflict resolver if a conflict is detected.

fields.name: conflict_detected
fields.description: Whether a conflict was detected by conflict resolver.

types.description: Result from a single SpanAnalyzer. Each analyzer populates the fields relevant to its domain; other fields remain at defaults. Composed into ArbiterEnrichment by the dispatcher.

types.name: OptionalFloat
types.description: Optional float value.

types.name: OptionalBool
types.description: Optional bool value.

types.name: OptionalTrustTier
types.description: Optional TrustTier.

types.name: OptionalBlastTier
types.description: Optional BlastTier.

types.name: StringList
types.description: List of strings.

types.name: ArbiterEnrichment

fields.name: access_declared
fields.description: arbiter.access.declared — declared access pattern from manifest/authority.

fields.name: access_observed
fields.description: arbiter.access.observed — observed access pattern from adapter ground truth.

fields.name: consistency
fields.description: arbiter.consistency — consistency score in range [0.0, 1.0].

fields.name: trust_score
fields.description: arbiter.trust.score — raw trust score used for all policy calculations. Trust is earned, computed from ledger.

fields.name: trust_tier
fields.description: arbiter.trust.tier — display tier only, never used in policy calculations.

fields.name: authority_domains
fields.description: arbiter.authority.domains — declared authority domains. Authority is declared, distinct from trust.

fields.name: taint_detected
fields.description: arbiter.taint.detected — whether taint propagation was detected.

fields.name: blast_tier
fields.description: arbiter.blast.tier — blast radius classification tier.

fields.name: conflict
fields.description: arbiter.conflict — conflict description if detected, empty string if no conflict.

types.description: Frozen Pydantic model containing all 9 arbiter.* enrichment attributes (C012). Composed (not inherited) with ParsedSpan into EnrichedSpan. Aggregated from AnalysisResult instances from all 4 analyzers.

types.name: EnrichedSpan

fields.name: span
fields.description: The original parsed and validated Baton span.

fields.name: enrichment
fields.description: All 9 arbiter.* enrichment attributes computed by the analyzer pipeline.

fields.name: enriched_at_unix_nano
fields.description: Timestamp when enrichment completed, nanoseconds since Unix epoch (UTC).

fields.name: analyzer_errors
fields.description: Errors from individual analyzers that failed. Enrichment still produced with defaults for failed analyzers.

types.description: Composition of ParsedSpan + ArbiterEnrichment. This is the final output of the subscriber pipeline, ready for downstream consumers. Composition over inheritance per project standards.

types.name: AnalyzerError

fields.name: analyzer_kind
fields.description: Which analyzer failed.

fields.name: error_type
fields.description: Exception class name.

fields.name: error_message
fields.description: Error detail including specific node/field/domain per project standards.

fields.name: span_id
fields.description: SpanId of the span that caused the failure.

fields.name: node_id
fields.description: Baton node ID from the span for error context.

types.description: Record of a single analyzer failure during dispatch. Failure isolation: one analyzer failure does not block others.

types.name: AnalyzerErrorList
types.description: List of AnalyzerError.

types.name: ParsedSpanList
types.description: List of ParsedSpan.

types.name: EnrichedSpanList
types.description: List of EnrichedSpan.

types.name: ExportResult

fields.name: accepted_count
fields.description: Number of spans successfully parsed, enriched, and dispatched.

fields.name: rejected_count
fields.description: Number of spans rejected due to validation errors (not warnings).

fields.name: enriched_spans
fields.description: Successfully enriched spans.

fields.name: validation_issues
fields.description: All validation issues (warnings and errors) across the batch.

fields.name: partial_success_message
fields.description: Human-readable summary for ExportTraceServiceResponse.partial_success.error_message.

types.description: Result of processing an ExportTraceServiceRequest batch. Maps to ExportTraceServiceResponse partial_success semantics: never reject entire batches.

types.name: BufferStats

fields.name: current_size
fields.description: Number of ExecutionId keys currently in the buffer.

fields.name: total_spans
fields.description: Total number of ParsedSpan instances across all buffer entries.

fields.name: max_size
fields.description: Configured maximum number of keys.

fields.name: total_evictions
fields.description: Cumulative count of keys evicted since buffer creation.

fields.name: total_window_misses
fields.description: Cumulative count of lookups that found an execution ID but all its spans had expired.

fields.name: oldest_entry_age_seconds
fields.description: Age of oldest entry in seconds. None if buffer is empty.

types.description: Metrics snapshot from the ExecutionCorrelationBuffer for observability.

types.name: AnalysisResultList
types.description: List of AnalysisResult from all dispatched analyzers.

functions.name: load_config
functions.description: Load OtlpSubscriberConfig from a YAML file via pathlib. Validates all fields via Pydantic. Returns default config if path does not exist or is empty.

inputs.name: config_path
inputs.description: Path to YAML configuration file. Resolved via pathlib.Path.

error_cases.name: invalid_yaml
error_cases.condition: File exists but contains invalid YAML syntax

error_data.includes: file path and parse error detail

error_cases.name: validation_failure
error_cases.condition: YAML parses but field values fail Pydantic validation

error_data.includes: specific field name and constraint that failed

error_cases.name: permission_denied
error_cases.condition: File exists but is not readable

postconditions: Returned config has all fields populated (defaults applied where not specified in YAML)
All validator constraints are satisfied on the returned config

structured_side_effects.description: Reads configuration from filesystem via pathlib

functions.name: start
functions.description: Start the gRPC server, correlation buffer eviction daemon, and begin accepting OTLP span exports. Binds to configured address:port. Transitions status from NOT_STARTED or STOPPED to SERVING. Registers TraceServiceServicer and gRPC health checking service. Target: accepting connections in under 3 seconds.

inputs.name: analyzers
inputs.description: Dict mapping AnalyzerKind to SpanAnalyzer implementations. All 4 must be registered.

error_cases.name: port_in_use
error_cases.condition: Configured port is already bound by another process

error_cases.name: already_running
error_cases.condition: Server status is STARTING or SERVING

error_cases.name: missing_analyzer
error_cases.condition: One or more of the 4 required AnalyzerKind entries is missing from analyzers

error_cases.name: bind_failure
error_cases.condition: Cannot bind to specified address (e.g. invalid address, permission denied)

error_data.includes: bind_address, port, and OS error detail

preconditions: Server status is NOT_STARTED or STOPPED
analyzers contains all 4 AnalyzerKind entries (CONSISTENCY, ACCESS, TAINT, CONFLICT)
postconditions: gRPC server is listening on config.bind_address:config.port
Server status is SERVING
gRPC health check reports SERVING
Correlation buffer eviction daemon thread is running
ThreadPoolExecutor has config.max_workers threads

structured_side_effects.description: Starts listening for OTLP ExportTraceServiceRequest RPCs

structured_side_effects.target: Server status, ThreadPoolExecutor, correlation buffer daemon
structured_side_effects.description: Initializes server state and background threads
Logs server start with port, max_workers, buffer config

functions.name: stop
functions.description: Gracefully stop the gRPC server. Drains correlation buffer, waits for in-flight RPCs up to grace period, then forces shutdown. Transitions status to STOPPED.

inputs.name: grace
inputs.description: Grace period in seconds. If None, uses config.grace_shutdown_seconds.

error_cases.name: not_running
error_cases.condition: Server status is NOT_STARTED or STOPPED

error_cases.name: shutdown_timeout
error_cases.condition: In-flight RPCs did not complete within grace period

error_data.includes: number of in-flight RPCs, grace period seconds

preconditions: Server status is SERVING or STARTING
postconditions: Server status is STOPPED
gRPC server is no longer accepting connections
ThreadPoolExecutor is shut down
Correlation buffer eviction daemon has stopped
All buffered spans have been drained (best-effort)

structured_side_effects.description: Stops accepting new connections, drains in-flight RPCs
Logs shutdown initiation, drain results, and completion

functions.name: is_ready
functions.description: Check whether the subscriber is ready to accept spans. Returns true only when server status is SERVING and all 4 analyzers are registered.
postconditions: Return value is true if and only if server status is SERVING and all 4 analyzers are registered

functions.name: get_server_status
functions.description: Return the current ServerStatus of the gRPC server.
postconditions: Returned status reflects the actual current lifecycle state

functions.name: parse_and_validate_spans
functions.description: Anti-corruption layer: convert raw protobuf span data (represented as list of dicts from OTLP deserialization) into validated ParsedSpan domain models. Performs BatonSpanContract schema validation. Logs structured warnings for unexpected/missing attributes with specific node, field, domain. Does not crash on validation warnings — only validation errors cause span rejection. Handles protobuf default-value ambiguity (0/empty != unset).

inputs.name: raw_spans
inputs.description: List of dicts representing deserialized protobuf Span messages from ExportTraceServiceRequest.

inputs.name: resource_attributes
inputs.description: Resource-level attributes from the ResourceSpans envelope, applied to all spans in this batch.

error_cases.name: malformed_protobuf
error_cases.condition: A raw span dict is structurally invalid (not a dict, missing required top-level keys)

error_cases.name: trace_id_invalid
error_cases.condition: trace_id is not 32 hex chars or is all zeros

error_cases.name: span_id_invalid
error_cases.condition: span_id is not 16 hex chars or is all zeros

error_cases.name: missing_baton_node_id
error_cases.condition: Required baton.node_id attribute is absent from span

error_cases.name: missing_baton_execution_id
error_cases.condition: Required baton.execution_id attribute is absent from span

error_cases.name: timestamp_zero
error_cases.condition: start_time_unix_nano or end_time_unix_nano is 0 (protobuf default, not explicitly set)

error_data.includes: span_id, which timestamp field is zero

preconditions: raw_spans is a list (may be empty)
postconditions: Every span in parsed_spans has passed BatonSpanContract validation (possibly with warnings)
Every span in rejected_spans has at least one ERROR-severity ValidationIssue
parsed_spans.length + rejected_count == raw_spans.length
All timestamps in parsed spans are positive (not protobuf default 0)
duration_ns == end_time_unix_nano - start_time_unix_nano for every parsed span
received_at_unix_nano is set to current UTC time at parse boundary

structured_side_effects.description: Logs WARNING-level structured messages for each validation issue with node, field, domain specifics

functions.name: dispatch_to_analyzers
functions.description: Dispatch a ParsedSpan to all 4 registered analyzers (consistency, access, taint, conflict) with failure isolation. Each analyzer is invoked independently; if one throws an exception, it is caught, logged, and processing continues with the remaining analyzers. Returns aggregated AnalysisResult list.

inputs.name: span
inputs.description: The validated parsed span to analyze.

preconditions: All 4 analyzers are registered (CONSISTENCY, ACCESS, TAINT, CONFLICT)
span has passed validation (is a valid ParsedSpan)
postconditions: Returned list has exactly 4 entries, one per AnalyzerKind
For each analyzer that threw an exception: success=false, error_message populated with node/field detail
For each analyzer that completed: success=true, relevant fields populated
No exception is propagated to caller — all failures are captured in results

structured_side_effects.description: Logs ERROR-level message for each analyzer that throws, including analyzer kind, span_id, node_id, exception detail

functions.name: aggregate_enrichment
functions.description: Aggregate AnalysisResult instances from all 4 analyzers into a single ArbiterEnrichment (frozen Pydantic model). Applies defaults for any analyzer that failed. Enrichment is always produced even if some analyzers errored.

inputs.name: results
inputs.description: Exactly 4 AnalysisResult instances, one per AnalyzerKind.

error_cases.name: wrong_result_count
error_cases.condition: results does not have exactly 4 entries

error_cases.name: duplicate_analyzer_kind
error_cases.condition: Two or more results have the same AnalyzerKind

preconditions: Each AnalyzerKind appears exactly once in results
postconditions: All 9 arbiter.* attributes are populated
consistency is in [0.0, 1.0]
trust_score is in [0.0, 1.0]
trust_tier is for display only — reflects trust_score but is never used in policy
For failed analyzers: corresponding fields have safe defaults (0.0 for scores, UNKNOWN for tiers, empty for strings, false for booleans)

functions.name: build_enriched_span
functions.description: Compose a ParsedSpan and ArbiterEnrichment into an EnrichedSpan. Records enrichment timestamp and any analyzer errors. Pure function — no side effects.

inputs.name: analyzer_errors
inputs.description: Errors from failed analyzers, if any.

postconditions: enriched_span.span is the input span (identity)
enriched_span.enrichment is the input enrichment (identity)
enriched_span.enriched_at_unix_nano is set to current UTC time
enriched_span.analyzer_errors matches input analyzer_errors

functions.name: handle_export_request
functions.description: Top-level handler for ExportTraceServiceRequest. Orchestrates the full pipeline: parse → buffer → dispatch → aggregate → enrich. Returns ExportResult with partial_success semantics — never rejects entire batches. This is the entry point called by the gRPC TraceServiceServicer.Export method.

inputs.name: raw_spans
inputs.description: List of dicts representing deserialized protobuf Span messages.

error_cases.name: server_not_ready
error_cases.condition: Server status is not SERVING

error_cases.name: batch_too_large
error_cases.condition: raw_spans length exceeds config.max_batch_size

error_data.includes: actual size, max_batch_size — excess spans are rejected, rest processed

error_cases.name: empty_batch
error_cases.condition: raw_spans is empty

error_data.includes: warning only, returns ExportResult with zero counts

postconditions: accepted_count + rejected_count == len(raw_spans)
Each accepted span is enriched with all 9 arbiter.* attributes
Each accepted span has been added to the correlation buffer
Each accepted span has been dispatched to all 4 analyzers
Partial success: if some spans fail validation, remaining spans are still processed
validation_issues contains all issues across the batch (warnings and errors)

structured_side_effects.description: Adds parsed spans to correlation buffer keyed by execution ID
Logs batch processing summary: accepted, rejected, validation issues, analyzer errors

functions.name: buffer_add
functions.description: Add a parsed span to the ExecutionCorrelationBuffer, keyed by its baton_execution_id. Thread-safe via threading.Lock. Triggers lazy eviction if buffer exceeds max_size.

inputs.name: span
inputs.description: The parsed span to buffer for correlation.

error_cases.name: buffer_full_no_evictable
error_cases.condition: Buffer is at max_size and no entries are eligible for eviction (all within window)

error_data.includes: current size, max_size, oldest entry age

postconditions: Span is retrievable via buffer_get(span.baton_execution_id) until evicted
If buffer_max_size was exceeded, at least one expired or oldest entry was evicted
Buffer size metrics are updated

structured_side_effects.description: Inserts span under execution_id key, may evict expired entries
Logs at DEBUG level on add; logs at WARN level on forced eviction

functions.name: buffer_get
functions.description: Retrieve all buffered spans for a given execution ID from the correlation buffer. Thread-safe. Returns empty list if execution ID not found or all spans expired.

inputs.name: execution_id
inputs.description: The execution ID to look up.

postconditions: Returned list contains only spans within the configured window_duration_seconds
Returned list is ordered by received_at_unix_nano ascending
If execution_id not in buffer, returns empty list
Window miss metric incremented if key exists but all spans expired

structured_side_effects.description: Increments window miss counter if key found but all spans expired

functions.name: buffer_get_stats
functions.description: Return a snapshot of correlation buffer metrics for observability.
postconditions: Returned stats reflect buffer state at time of call
current_size <= max_size

functions.name: buffer_drain
functions.description: Drain all entries from the correlation buffer. Called during graceful shutdown. Returns all buffered spans for final processing.
postconditions: Buffer is empty after drain
All previously buffered spans are in the returned list
Buffer stats show current_size == 0

structured_side_effects.description: Clears all entries from the buffer
Logs drain operation with count of drained entries and spans

functions.name: buffer_evict_expired
functions.description: Evict all buffer entries whose spans have all aged past window_duration_seconds. Called lazily on insert and periodically by the daemon sweep thread. Thread-safe.
postconditions: All entries where every span is older than window_duration_seconds have been removed
total_evictions metric incremented by returned count
Returned int is the number of execution_id keys evicted

structured_side_effects.target: ExecutionCorrelationBuffer internal dict and eviction counter
structured_side_effects.description: Removes expired entries and updates metrics
Logs at DEBUG level with eviction count if > 0

consistency_analyzer: Implements SpanAnalyzer Protocol: analyze(span: ParsedSpan) -> AnalysisResult
Must be stateless or internally thread-safe — called from grpcio thread pool threads
Returns AnalysisResult with consistency_score float and optional findings list
access_auditor: Implements SpanAnalyzer Protocol: analyze(span: ParsedSpan) -> AnalysisResult
Must be stateless or internally thread-safe — called from grpcio thread pool threads
Returns AnalysisResult with declared_access, observed_access, and authority_domains
taint_detector: Implements SpanAnalyzer Protocol: analyze(span: ParsedSpan) -> AnalysisResult
Must be stateless or internally thread-safe — called from grpcio thread pool threads
Returns AnalysisResult with taint_detected bool and taint details
conflict_resolver: Implements SpanAnalyzer Protocol: analyze(span: ParsedSpan) -> AnalysisResult
Must be stateless or internally thread-safe — called from grpcio thread pool threads
Returns AnalysisResult with conflict description if detected
May request correlated spans from correlation buffer via ExecutionId

invariants: Trust and authority are distinct: trust is earned (computed from ledger), authority is declared (from manifests) — never conflated in any enrichment or analysis
The adapter layer is ground truth; node self-reports are claims — baton_node_id from adapter attributes is authoritative
All policy calculations use raw trust_score (float), never trust_tier (enum) — trust_tier is display/enrichment only
Partial success semantics: the server never rejects an entire ExportTraceServiceRequest batch — valid spans are always processed even if some spans in the batch fail validation
The correlation buffer is append-only within a window: spans are never modified after insertion, only evicted by age or capacity
Failure isolation: a single analyzer failure never prevents other analyzers from processing the same span — all 4 are always invoked
All timestamps are UTC, represented as nanoseconds since Unix epoch — no local time, no timezone-naive datetimes

Protobuf default-value ambiguity is handled explicitly: 0 for int fields and empty string for string fields are treated as 'unset' where semantically appropriate (e.g. start_time_unix_nano == 0 is invalid)
The correlation buffer is bounded: it never exceeds buffer_max_size keys — lazy eviction on insert plus periodic sweep guarantee this
All protobuf-to-domain conversion happens at the handler boundary — no protobuf types leak past parse_and_validate_spans
Server transitions to SERVING only when all 4 analyzers are registered and gRPC is accepting connections — is_ready() reflects this
Error messages always include the specific node, field, or domain that caused the error per project standards
Thread safety: correlation buffer operations are serialized via threading.Lock — no asyncio (AS002)
Cold start: server accepting connections in under 3 seconds from start() invocation
